summary: PostCSS-based responsive breakpoint system with component-level mobile-first optimizations and custom media query shortcuts

breakpointSystem.philosophy: Mobile-first responsive design with components that adapt automatically
breakpointSystem.approach: Components handle responsive behavior internally - developers trust defaults

customMediaQueries.description: PostCSS transforms custom media query shortcuts to standard breakpoints during build

componentBehaviors: GoabBlock: direction='column' on mobile regardless of prop

notMobile.description: Tablet and desktop combined (everything except mobile)

notTablet.description: Mobile and desktop combined (skip tablet-specific rules)
notTablet.useCases: Rare - used when tablet needs special handling different from mobile AND desktop

notDesktop.description: Mobile and tablet combined (everything except desktop)

postcssTransformation.description: Build-time transformation of custom media queries to standard CSS
postcssTransformation.benefit: Developers write readable shortcuts, browsers receive standard media queries

componentResponsiveBehaviors.description: Components automatically adapt to viewport - no custom media queries needed

goabButton.override: Use width prop to force specific width on any viewport

goabFormItem.desktop: Label beside input (horizontal) when space allows

props.gap: spacing token (xs, s, m, l, xl, etc.)

goabBlock.mobileBehavior: Often forces column direction on mobile regardless of prop
goabBlock.desktopBehavior: Respects direction prop for row/column layouts

goabGrid.behavior: Creates as many columns as fit based on minChildWidth, automatically wraps to fewer columns on smaller screens

goabOneColumnLayout.description: Mandatory page wrapper - responsive by design

trustComponentDefaults.rule: Components are already responsive - don't write custom media queries
trustComponentDefaults.reasoning: GoA components handle breakpoints internally for consistency

useLayoutComponents.rule: Use GoabBlock and GoabGrid for responsive layouts

avoidCustomMediaQueries.rule: DO NOT write @media queries in application code
reasoning: Custom media queries break design system consistency
Difficult to maintain across viewport sizes
May conflict with component internal styles
avoidCustomMediaQueries.ifYouMust: If you absolutely need custom responsive behavior, use layout components (GoabBlock, GoabGrid) instead

mobileFirstMindset.principle: Design for mobile first, enhance for larger screens
approach: Test on actual mobile devices, not just browser resize

realDevices.importance: Browser emulation is helpful but not perfect
realDevices.recommendation: Test on actual mobile devices when possible

touchTargets.implementation: GoA components meet this by default

textSize.mobile: Minimum 16px base font size to prevent zoom
textSize.desktop: Can use smaller sizes for dense information
textSize.tokens: Use typography tokens (body-s, body-m, body-l) which are responsive

viewportMeta.reasoning: Enables proper responsive behavior on mobile browsers

extractingBreakpoints.description: Figma designs usually show desktop OR mobile - not both
extractingBreakpoints.approach: Generate code that works on all viewports using GoA components
strategy: 1. Identify if Figma shows mobile or desktop design
2. Use GoA components that auto-adapt (don't hardcode viewport-specific code)
4. Use layout components (Block, Grid) for structure

mobileDesigns.codeGeneration: Generate using standard GoA components - they'll work on desktop too

desktopDesigns.codeGeneration: Use GoabGrid or GoabBlock with direction='row' - they'll adapt to mobile

twoColumnToSingleColumn.description: Desktop shows side-by-side, mobile stacks vertically

implementation.alternative: <GoabBlock direction='row' gap='m'><!-- Automatically stacks on mobile --></GoabBlock>

conditionalNavigation.description: Different navigation patterns for mobile vs desktop
conditionalNavigation.avoidCustom: Don't build custom nav with media queries

fullWidthMobile.description: Buttons/inputs full-width on mobile, auto on desktop
fullWidthMobile.solution: GoabButton and GoabInput do this by default

checklist: Are you using actual GoA components (not custom implementations)?
Are you overriding component styles with custom CSS?
Did you test in actual browser/device (not just resize)?
Is the parent container constraining the component?

layoutBreaking.issue: Layout breaks on certain viewport sizes
solutions: Use GoabBlock or GoabGrid instead of custom flex/grid
Check for hardcoded widths in parent containers
Verify you're not fighting component defaults with custom CSS
Test with browser dev tools at exact breakpoints (623px, 1024px)

noCustomMediaQueries.rule: NEVER write @media queries in application code
noCustomMediaQueries.always: Use GoA components' built-in responsive behavior
noCustomMediaQueries.reasoning: Maintains consistency and leverages tested component behaviors

trustComponentDefaults.rule: Components know how to respond to viewport changes
trustComponentDefaults.always: Use components as-is without viewport-specific logic
trustComponentDefaults.exception: Only override if you have specific government service requirements

mobileFirst.rule: Design and code for mobile first
mobileFirst.reasoning: Easier to enhance for desktop than to constrain for mobile
mobileFirst.implementation: GoA components follow mobile-first by default

relatedKnowledge.components: See component definition files for specific responsive behaviors
relatedKnowledge.layout: See layout.json for GoabBlock, GoabGrid, GoabOneColumnLayout details
